fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 13-year-old female brought by mother for brief reactive sadness after pet death.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 13-year-old female presenting three days after the death of her dog of 10 years. She reports feeling very sad and crying frequently, particularly in the evenings. She denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states her dog was her best friend and she misses him constantly. Appetite is slightly decreased and she has difficulty falling asleep due to sadness. She attended school today but was tearful during lunch. Mother reports patient created a memorial for the dog and has been looking at photos. Patient denies regrets about the decision to humanely euthanize the dog due to terminal illness and states she was able to say goodbye. She has been talking with friends who have also lost pets, which provides some comfort. (Social Worker Eric Taylor, LCSW)
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents and younger sibling; supportive family; strong peer network; recently lost beloved pet; academically and socially successful; healthy coping with loss.
Assessment_and_Plan: Normal grief reaction following pet death; no pathology identified.
Protective factors: supportive family, peer support, healthy grieving process, ability to discuss feelings, no suicidal ideation.
Reassurance provided regarding normal grief timeline.
Encouraged continued expression of feelings and memorial activities.
Resources provided for pet loss support groups if desired.
Mother encouraged to monitor but expect natural resolution over coming weeks.
School counselor may provide additional support if needed.
No intervention required; follow-up only if symptoms worsen or persist beyond expected timeframe.
fields.Author: Eric Taylor, LCSW (Social Worker)
